cta.title: Curious About What Natural Therapies Can Do for You?
cta.subtitle: Schedule a consultation to explore which treatments align with your health goals. Together, we'll create a care plan shaped around your individual needs.

faqs.answer: Acupuncture is generally well-tolerated, with most people experiencing little to no discomfort. Because the needles are hair-thin — far finer than standard injection needles — insertion usually feels like a brief, mild sensation, or nothing at all. Once the needles are placed, patients often find themselves deeply relaxed, and some even drift off to sleep during the session.
faqs.question: Does acupuncture hurt?

faqs.answer: Treatment frequency depends on your specific condition, how long you've been experiencing it, and how your body responds over time. Acute concerns may show improvement within 3–6 sessions, whereas chronic conditions often call for 10–15 treatments or more. We'll put together a personalized treatment plan from the start and refine it as we track your progress.
faqs.question: How many sessions will I need?

faqs.answer: When delivered by a qualified, licensed practitioner, Chinese medicine is considered safe. We use sterile, single-use needles and adhere to strict safety protocols throughout every treatment. Our Chinese herbal medicine formulas are sourced from reputable suppliers and undergo quality testing. Adverse effects are uncommon and, when they do occur, tend to be minor.
faqs.question: Is Chinese medicine a safe option?

faqs.answer: TCM and Western medicine can work very well together. Many patients turn to acupuncture to help manage side effects from medications or medical procedures, to support recovery, or to address concerns that conventional approaches haven't fully resolved. We always encourage open, ongoing communication between you and all of your healthcare providers.
faqs.question: Can I use TCM alongside my existing Western medical care?

faqs.answer: Choose loose, comfortable clothing that can be rolled up easily to allow access to your arms, legs, and back. Gowns are available if you need them. Please also skip perfume or scented products on treatment days, as some patients may have sensitivities.
faqs.question: What should I wear to my treatment appointment?

faqs.answer: We work with most major insurance plans that provide acupuncture coverage. If you'd like to seek reimbursement, we can issue a superbill — a detailed receipt you can submit directly to your insurer. Reach out to us to confirm your specific benefits. For those paying out of pocket, we offer accessible self-pay rates as well as package discounts.
faqs.question: Does your practice accept insurance?

faqs.answer: Have a light meal 1–2 hours before your visit — arriving on an empty stomach isn't recommended. On treatment days, limit alcohol and excess caffeine. Bring a list of any current medications or supplements, and wear comfortable clothing. Plan to share your health history in some detail, and feel free to come with any questions you have in mind.
faqs.question: How do I prepare for my first appointment?

faqs.answer: It's very common to feel apprehensive about needles, and many patients are surprised by how comfortable acupuncture actually is. The needles are extremely fine, and insertion is typically painless. We'll move at a pace that feels right for you, walk you through each step, and can begin with fewer needles if that helps. We also offer needle-free options, including herbal medicine, cupping, and Tui Na massage.
faqs.question: What if needles make me nervous?

overview.introduction: Dr. Maya Chen Clinic provides a full spectrum of Traditional Chinese Medicine treatments, each thoughtfully chosen and adapted to your specific health concerns and wellness goals. Our approach brings together time-honored practice and contemporary understanding, offering natural, effective care that addresses the whole person.

items.title: Individualized Treatment Plans
items.description: Built around your personal health needs and goals

items.title: Safe & Professional Care
items.description: Consistent, high-quality treatment in a clean clinical environment

items.title: Acupuncture
benefits: Supports pain management without pharmaceutical intervention
May help ease stress and physical tension
Can contribute to better sleep quality
Helps promote healthy circulation and the body's recovery process
items.whatToExpect: When you visit Acupuncture New York, your care begins with a thorough intake, followed by thoughtful point selection and a restful phase while the needles are in place. You'll leave with a clear treatment plan, a sense of the pacing ahead, and guidance for follow-up. Most patients experience very little discomfort and often feel deeply relaxed throughout the session.
items.fullDescription:
At Acupuncture New York, acupuncture is delivered with a structured, family-centered, and genuinely caring approach.

By stimulating specific points on the body, acupuncture works to help regulate circulation, calm the nervous system, and encourage the body's own recovery mechanisms.

Every treatment plan is built around you — shaped by your symptoms, health history, and how you respond as care progresses.
items.shortDescription: A foundational Traditional Chinese Medicine practice that uses fine, sterile needles to help with pain relief, stress regulation, and overall balance. Each treatment is individualized to address both immediate symptoms and longer-term wellbeing.

items.title: Chinese Herbal Medicine
benefits: Individually tailored formulation approach
Designed to help address chronic and functional health concerns
Works well alongside acupuncture treatment
Grounded in pattern-based diagnostic principles
items.whatToExpect: At Acupuncture New York, your care is thoughtfully planned from the start — with clear treatment pacing and ongoing follow-up support. Following an initial assessment, you'll receive a formula crafted specifically for your needs, along with straightforward dosing instructions. Return visits allow your practitioner to track your progress and refine the prescription as your condition evolves.
items.fullDescription:
At Acupuncture New York, Chinese Herbal Medicine is practiced with warmth, structure, and a family-centered philosophy.

Formulas in Chinese herbal medicine bring together multiple botanical ingredients in carefully balanced combinations.

As your condition shifts, prescriptions are refined accordingly — with close attention to how your symptoms, digestion, sleep, and energy levels are responding over time.
items.shortDescription: Herbal formulas crafted to align with your individual presentation, constitution, and health goals. Care is personalized to address both current symptoms and support lasting balance over time.

items.title: Cupping Therapy
benefits: Assists in releasing tension held in the muscles
Helps encourage circulation in targeted areas
Can help ease stiffness that follows physical strain
May offer support for respiratory comfort
items.whatToExpect: At Acupuncture New York, each session comes with a clear plan, thoughtful pacing, and follow-up guidance tailored to you. During treatment, you may notice a firm pulling or pressure sensation. Circular marks on the skin are common and typically fade on their own within a few days.
items.fullDescription:
At Acupuncture New York, Cupping Therapy is offered within a warm, family-focused, and well-structured care environment.

Using negative pressure, cupping mobilizes soft tissue and encourages improved blood flow to the treated area.

Depending on your condition and comfort level, your practitioner may use static or moving cup techniques.
items.shortDescription: A suction-based approach that works to release muscle tightness, encourage circulation, and relieve discomfort linked to stagnation. Treatment is shaped around your specific needs, with attention to both short-term relief and ongoing balance.

items.title: Moxibustion
benefits: Delivers targeted warmth to support healing
Can help relieve cold-type discomfort patterns
Helps encourage circulation and overall vitality
Pairs effectively with acupuncture treatment protocols
items.whatToExpect: At Acupuncture New York, you'll receive a clear care plan, well-paced treatment, and follow-up support throughout your journey. During moxibustion, you'll feel a gentle, steady warmth as your practitioner monitors closely to keep you comfortable and safe.
items.fullDescription:
At Acupuncture New York, Moxibustion is provided within a caring, family-oriented, and thoughtfully structured practice.

Therapeutic heat is applied to selected acupuncture points using moxibustion to warm the channels and encourage the movement of qi.

This technique is frequently used to address cold sensitivity, low energy, and certain patterns associated with chronic conditions.
items.shortDescription: A heat-based therapy that applies mugwort to specific points, supporting circulation, energy, and conditions rooted in cold patterns. Each treatment plan is adapted to your individual needs, balancing symptom relief with long-term wellbeing.

items.title: Tui Na Medical Massage
benefits: Helps improve mobility in joints and soft tissue
Can ease tension in the neck, shoulders, and back
May support recovery following overuse or repetitive strain
Combines naturally with acupuncture as part of a broader care plan
items.whatToExpect: At Acupuncture New York, every patient receives a clear treatment plan, thoughtfully paced sessions, and ongoing follow-up guidance. The pressure used during Tui Na Medical Massage can be adjusted — from gentle to more firm — based on your comfort and what your clinical goals call for.
items.fullDescription:
At Acupuncture New York, Tui Na Medical Massage is delivered with warmth, clinical structure, and a family-centered approach to care.

Drawing on specific hand techniques applied along channels and affected areas, Tui Na works to relieve muscular tension, restore ease of movement, and support your rehabilitation objectives.
items.shortDescription: A hands-on Chinese medicine bodywork practice that combines acupressure and mobilization techniques to address pain and support physical function. Treatment is shaped around both your immediate concerns and your longer-term health goals.
